name: C(12,6,4) matched cardinality encoding benchmark
hypothesis: On the fixed 20-leaf sample, kmtotalizer produces at least 8 replay-verified UNSAT closures and at least four more replay-verified closures than sequential under identical 60-second cold CaDiCaL caps.
expected_signal: All non-cardinality core hashes and audits agree; every UNSAT proof replays; kmtotalizer closes at least 8/20 and at least four more leaves than sequential; projected proofs stay below 30 GB.
decision_value: Determines whether the 50.17% auxiliary reduction materially improves certified closure on the inherited 47-node canonical link frontier before any scale-up.

efficiency_design.naive_cost: The unresolved link search contains 47 canonical frontier nodes, each with 462 primary block variables; an unchanged 60-second solve of both encodings over all nodes would allocate up to 94 CPU-minutes before proof replay and would not first measure whether the encoding change helps.
opportunities_considered: retain the exact C2 wr S5 canonical root, secondary, and tertiary quotient
stratify a fixed 20/47 route-discriminator sample rather than traverse all frontier nodes
byte-hash one common non-cardinality core so only the 11 degree equalities differ
use isolated auxiliary ranges and boundary-semantic checks for each point equality
run independent cold processes to avoid solver-state and learned-clause confounding
checkpoint after every solver run and replay every UNSAT proof immediately
directly validate and canonicalize every SAT link against the full four-orbit catalog
stop when observed proof sizes project beyond 30 GB
efficiency_design.chosen_reductions: Use the already proved 3,840-element link stabilizer and 47-node canonical frontier, then test 20 fixed leaves (6 secondary and 14 tertiary) with a matched two-encoding cold design. Reuse clause generation, not solver state. The kmtotalizer form uses 20,020 degree auxiliaries versus 40,180 for sequential; both preserve the exact same primary-variable core.
efficiency_design.expected_throughput_gain: The discriminator uses at most 40 solver-minutes, 42.6% of an all-frontier paired pass. Kmtotalizer removes 20,160 auxiliaries (50.17% of the sequential auxiliaries) while adding 853 clauses; continuation requires at least 8/20 verified closures and an advantage of at least four over sequential.
efficiency_design.soundness_basis: Each leaf is bound to the inherited 47-node manifest by exact indices and result hashes. A separate checker reconstructs the 165 triple clauses, 1,616 catalog blockers, canonical units, and all point literal universes; it checks auxiliary confinement and 66 boundary assignments per CNF. Every UNSAT must have a DRAT proof replayed by pinned DRAT-trim. SAT is accepted only after direct exact-link validation and full-catalog canonicalization.
efficiency_design.remains_uncompressed: The sample is a route discriminator, not exhaustive coverage and not an orbit-weighted closure estimate. If the acceptance rule passes, the remaining 27 frontier nodes and any still-open sample leaves require a new separately reviewed tranche; if it fails, this encoding route stops unchanged.
